findings.title: Netlify lead function returns raw internal error message to client
findings.description: Unlike server/server.js and api/telegram-lead.js (which return a generic 'Lead delivery failed'), the Netlify handler returns error.message verbatim to the caller. Leaked strings include 'Telegram credentials are not configured', 'Telegram delivery failed: <Telegram API description>', and raw JSON.parse SyntaxError text.
findings.exploit_scenario: Attacker POSTs malformed/empty bodies to the public /.netlify/functions/telegram-lead endpoint and reads the JSON error field to fingerprint backend state (whether Telegram creds are configured, upstream Telegram API responses, parser internals). No auth required.
findings.impact: Information disclosure of internal backend state. No secret/token is exposed (token never appears in any message), so impact is reconnaissance only.
findings.recommendation: Return a generic message like the other two handlers: json(error.statusCode || 500, { error: 'Lead delivery failed' }). Log the real error server-side only.
findings.verification: self-verified by reading netlify/functions/telegram-lead.js:29 and comparing to server/server.js:79 and api/telegram-lead.js:45

tools_skipped: npm audit (zero dependencies — no node_modules / lockfile)
